tweets.text: Nearly 1/3 of surveyed people in Anthropic now think entry-level engineers and researchers are likely replaced by Mythos within 3 months

Jensen: "What the fuck did you just fucking say about my ecosystem, you little bitch? I have 300 million confirmed GPUs, I'm trained in accelerated computing, and I will wipe you out with parallelized precision. You think you can get away with that loser premise? Think again,

LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning - 2,500 expert-designed problems spanning science, chess, and logic that demand up to hundreds of thousands of reasoning tokens - The best models achieve <10% accuracy

ByteDance presents Seedance 2.0 - Multi-modal inputs (text/image/audio/video) - #1 on Arena (both T2V + I2V) - Keeps characters consistent across complex scenes - Audio satisfaction: 62% vs <10% for others
proj: https:// seed.bytedance.com/en/seedance2_0 abs: https:// arxiv.org/abs/2604.14148 access: https:// volcengine.com/experience/ark ?mode=vision&modelId=doubao-seedance-2-0-260128&tab=GenVideo …

Together AI presents Introspective Diffusion LM - The first DLM to match the quality of AR while outperforming DLMs in both model quality and serving efficiency - Delivering about 3× higher throughput than prior SotA DLMs
Said hi to Dario in SF today got the usage limit warning before the handshake ended
Huawei proposes HiFloat4 FP4 (HiF4) format for Huawei Ascend NPUs and achieves performance comparable to BF16 baselines, while delivering substantial improvements in computational efficiency.

I regret spending 2.5 years on the application layer despite repeated advice from VCs and friends to focus on AI infra. I was wrong about that bet, and I’m shifting back to AI infra now. There’s less things left to do than in 2022, when I should’ve started, but it’s still

Squeeze Evolve: A Unified Framework for Verifier-Free Evolution Across AIME 2025, GPQA-Diamond, ARC-AGI-V2, MMMU-Pro, etc: - Up to ~3x API cost reduction - Up to ~10x increase in fixed-budget serving throughput

ClawBench: Can AI Agents Complete Everyday Online Tasks? A real-world benchmark for AI agents: 153 everyday online tasks across live websites (shopping, booking, job apps). Even top models struggle—dropping from ~70% on sandbox benchmarks to as low as 6.5% here.

People often get confused about API cost: open vs. closed, input vs. cache read vs. output. Per-token cost for Sonnet is like cache read : input (+ cache write) : output = $0.3 : ~$3 : $15. The real cost of running is likely much cheaper (>10×), at least you're effectively using
Collinear presents YC-Bench This benchmark evaluates agent capability to run a simulated startup over a one-year horizon spanning hundreds of turns.
proj: https:// collinear-ai.github.io/yc-bench/ abs: https:// arxiv.org/abs/2604.01212 repo: https:// github.com/collinear-ai/y c-bench …
Half of ~5k authors from 1.2k papers released in arXiv batch yesterday had Chinese last name. While research may be automated soon, this doesn't look very promising for the West.

abs: https:// arxiv.org/abs/2603.27164 repo: https:// github.com/GAIR-NLP/daVin ci-LLM … model: https:// huggingface.co/SII-GAIR-NLP/d avinci-llm-model … data: https:// huggingface.co/datasets/SII-G AIR-NLP/davinci-llm-data …

daVinci-LLM: Towards the Science of Pretraining - Matches larger model perf with half the size - Huge reasoning gains: +23 pts on MATH, strong code + science scores - Quality > scale: smarter data (not more data) drives major performance boosts

PRBench: End-to-end Paper Reproduction in Physics Research - Presents a benchmark of 30 expert-curated tasks spanning 11 subfields of physics - All agents exhibit a zero end-to-end callback success rate

LongCat-Next: Lexicalizing Modalities as Discrete Tokens - Matches or beats SOTA across multimodal benchmarks - SotA audio: strong on both recognition and TTS accuracy - No trade-offs: adds vision/audio without hurting core language performance
KwaiKAT presents KAT-Coder-V2 Presents KAT-Coder-V2, an agentic coding model that performs close to Claude Opus 4.6

While Alec is one of the best ML researchers of all time, LLM started way before. Here's one from 2013 for non-neural architecture and one from 2016, which is afaik the first neural LLM if we define LLM as LM w/ >1B params.
Meta presents AIRA2: Overcoming Bottlenecks in AI Research Agents Achieves SotA performance on MLE-bench-30

Automated near-term quantum algorithm discovery for molecular ground states LLM-powered system discovers quantum algorithms for molecular ground states, achieving chemical precision for LiH, H₂O, and F₂ with orders-of-magnitude reductions in circuit evaluations and gate counts
World Reasoning Arena - A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating world model - Expose a substantial gap between current models and human-level hypothetical reasoning

Last year, a few papers introduced “recipes” right before Claude Skills launched. Recipes are essentially agent-generated, reusable skill-like prompts that evolve over time ... more than just memory. Why do I not hear them much yet?

For about six months, my mom had severe hepatic encephalopathy—at times it looked like she might not survive. I’m not a doctor, but I kept detailed notes and ran them through GPT-5.x Pro for every new update. At one point it suggested spontaneous porto-systemic shunts (SPSS),

Interesting how people disagree on which models/tools are best for what. My stack: GPT-5.x Pro: general Q&A + brainstorming Figma Make: UI design Claude Code: everything else I’m a total Google fanboy and want Gemini to win, but rn it adds no Pareto value for me (idk why).

DeepSeek OCR dropped ... but honestly, Glyph [1], released the same day, showed something more interesting: 3–4× context compression and infilling cost reduction, no performance hit on long-context QA and summarization, which is much less trivial than OCR in many cases. If that

SWAX: short windows, long memory • Hybrid of sliding-window attn + xLSTM RNN • Counter-intuitive: shorter windows → better long-term recall • Fix: stochastic window sizes = strong short + long context performance • Outperforms fixed window attention

RLHI: Reinforcement Learning from Human Interaction • Moves beyond expert-annotated data → learns from real user conversations • Two methods: 1. User-Guided Rewrites 2. User-Based Rewards • Outperforms baselines in personalization, instruction-following & reasoning

ReasoningBank: memory for self-evolving LLM agents • Distills strategies from both successes & failures • Enables agents to learn, reuse, and improve over time • Outperforms prior memory methods on web & SWE tasks (+34.2% eff., –16% steps)

NVFP4: 4-bit pretraining for LLMs • New format w/ 2-level scaling + RHT + stochastic rounding • Trains 12B model on 10T tokens • Matches FP8 baseline: MMLU-pro 62.58% vs 62.62% • 6.8× efficiency boost potential → faster, cheaper frontier LLMs
Sutton’s Bitter Lesson: “scaling wins.” Also Sutton’s Bitter Lesson: now scaled into explaining literally everything. Over-credited. Over-applied. Bitter Law of Everything.

Microsoft presents Measuring LLM inference energy (production scale) • Median cost: 0.34 Wh/query (chatbot) • Long reasoning: 4.3 Wh/query (~13× higher) • Fleet scale: ~0.9 GWh/day @1B queries → ~web search level • Public est. often 4–20× too high • Efficiency gains

Veo 3 = Zero-shot video reasoner • Trained on web-scale video, shows broad zero-shot skills (perception → physics → manipulation → reasoning) • New “Chain-of-Frames” reasoning = visual analogue of CoT • Big jump Veo2 → Veo3: edits, memory, symmetry, mazes, analogies •

EmbeddingGemma: lightweight SOTA embeddings • 308M params, built on Gemma 3 • Tops MTEB (multilingual, English, code) <500M models • Matches models 2× its size, efficient even w/ 4-bit or 128-dim embeddings • Encoder-decoder init + geometric distillation • Spread-out

RT @DhruvBatraDB: Like clockwork, every few years we rediscover this lesson: on difficult tasks, VLMs regress to being nearly blind. Thi… https:// x.com/arankomatsuzak /arankomatsuzaki/status/1970684893966516477 …

Soft Tokens, Hard Truths • First scalable RL method for continuous CoT • Learns “soft” tokens (mixtures + noise) → richer reasoning paths • Matches discrete CoTs at pass@1, beats them at pass@32 (more diversity) • Best setup: train w/ soft tokens, infer w/ hard tokens

Effective reasoning ≠ longer CoTs • Across 10 LRMs: longer chains + more review → lower accuracy • New metric: Failed-Step Fraction (share of abandoned steps) predicts correctness best • FSF-based reranking boosts pass@1 by up to +10% • Editing out failed branches improves

The Illusion of Readiness (Health AI) • GPT-5 & peers ace med benchmarks—but stress tests reveal fragility • Guess answers w/o images, flip under trivial prompt tweaks • Fabricate “reasoning” that sounds right but isn’t • Leaderboard wins ≠ real-world readiness

RLPT: Reinforcement Learning on Pre-Training Data • RL directly on pre-train data (no human labels) • Next-segment reasoning objective (ASR + MSR tasks) → self-supervised rewards • Gains on Qwen3-4B: +3.0 MMLU, +8.1 GPQA-Diamond, +6.6 AIME24, +5.3 AIME25

Hierarchical Retrieval with Dual Encoders • DEs can solve HR if dim. scales with depth/log(# docs) • Challenge: lost-in-the-long-distance — far ancestors hard to retrieve • Solution: Pretrain → finetune on long-distance pairs • Boosts recall on WordNet from 19% → 76%
repo: https:// github.com/GAIR-NLP/LIMI model: https:// huggingface.co/GAIR/LIMI data: https:// huggingface.co/datasets/GAIR/ LIMI … abs:

LIMI: Less Is More for Agency • Argues agentic AI doesn’t need more data, just better data • 78 curated demos → 73.5% on AgencyBench (beats models trained on 10k samples) • Outperforms SOTA (Kimi-K2: 24.1%, DeepSeek: 11.9%, Qwen3: 27.5%, GLM-4.5: 45.1%) • Establishes Agency

EngDesign: Benchmarking LLMs toward Engineering AGI • 101 tasks across 9 domains (OS, circuits, robotics, etc.) • Simulation-based eval (SPICE, FEA, etc.), not just Q&A • Iterative refinement boosts pass rate (o3 → ~60%) • Error analysis: 111 failure modes identified
proj: https:// agi4engineering.github.io/Eng-Design/ data: https:// huggingface.co/datasets/opt1z er/EngDesign … repo: https:// github.com/AGI4Engineerin g/EngDesign … abs:

Meta Superintelligence Labs presents MetaEmbed: Scalable multimodal retrieval • Flexible late interaction via Meta Tokens • Test-time scaling: trade off retrieval accuracy vs efficiency • SOTA on MMEB + ViDoRe, robust up to 32B models • Matryoshka training → coarse-to-fine

DiffusionNFT: RL for diffusion models via the forward process • Contrastive fine-tuning: positives vs negatives → implicit policy improvement • Works with any solver, no CFG, no trajectory storage • 25× more efficient than FlowGRPO • Boosts SD3.5-M: GenEval 0.24 → 0.98 in

Nvidia presents ReaSyn: Rethinking molecule synthesizability • Treats synthesis like CoT with Chain-of-Reaction (CoR) steps • Each reaction = reasoning step → richer supervision & step-by-step learning • Adds RL finetuning + test-time scaling for better optimization • SOTA

Dynamic CFG: adaptive guidance for diffusion models • Static CFG = “one-size-fits-all” fails across prompts • New method: online feedback from latent evaluators (CLIP, fidelity, prefs) → dynamic per-step CFG • Just +1% overhead, big gains in alignment, quality & text

Apple presents Manzano: Simple & scalable unified multimodal LLM • Hybrid vision tokenizer (continuous discrete) cuts task conflict • SOTA on text-rich benchmarks, competitive in gen vs GPT-4o/Nano Banana • One model for both understanding & generation • Joint recipe:

SBP: Synthetic bootstrapped pretraining • Learns inter-document relations → synthesizes new training data • Goes beyond token-level correlations, capturing latent concepts • 3B model trained on 1T tokens: beats strong repetition baseline • Closes much of the gap to an

Latent Learning: why LLMs miss future-useful info • AI often fails at latent learning (e.g. reversal curse) • Cognitive science points to episodic memory as a fix • Oracle retrieval shows better generalization • Episodic memory + parametric learning = more human-like AI
Govt: H1B visas now cost $100k. Companies: guess we’ll just use AI and offshore workers. Startups: hold my pitch deck.

FlowRL: Reward Distribution Matching for LLM RL • Shifts from reward maximization → distribution matching • +10.0% vs GRPO, +5.1% vs PPO on math; strong gains on code • Minimizes reverse KL to cover all valid reasoning paths (avoids mode collapse)

Apple presents AToken: A unified visual tokenizer • First tokenizer unifying images, videos & 3D • Shared 4D latent space (preserves both reconstruction & semantics) • Strong across gen & understanding tasks (ImageNet 82.2%, MSRVTT 32.6%, 3D acc 90.9%)

SakanaAI presents Robust Agentic CUDA Kernel Optimization • Fuses ops, boosts forward/backward passes, outperforms torch baselines • Agentic LLM pipeline: PyTorch → CUDA → evolutionary runtime optimization • Soft-verification: LLMs flag incorrect kernels (↑30% verification

• Ensembles beat giant models → +5.17× data efficiency • Distillation keeps 83% of ensemble gains in 8× smaller models • Downstream: +9% avg, +17.5× data efficiency on math CPT wb: https:// wandb.ai/stanford-mercu ry/suhas-data-efficiency/reports/Pre-training-under-infinite-compute--VmlldzoxNDM5NzUzMQ … repo: https:// github.com/marin-communit y/marin/tree/suhas/data-efficiency … abs:

Pre-training under infinite compute • Data, not compute, is the new bottleneck • Standard recipes overfit → fix with strong regularization (30× weight decay) • Scaling laws: loss decreases monotonically, best measured by asymptote not fixed budget

Self-Improving Embodied FMs • 2-stage recipe: SFT + online RL w/ self-predicted rewards • Boosts sample efficiency: 10% robot time → 45%→75% success (vs. 8× data → only 60%) • Unlocks autonomous skill acquisition beyond imitation data

ScaleCUA: Scaling Open-Source Computer Use Agents • New agent family w/ 3 modes: Grounding, Direct Action, Reasoned Action • New SOTA on multiple GUI benchmarks with big gains: +26.6 (WebArena-Lite-v2), +10.7 (ScreenSpot-Pro),

• Massive cross-platform dataset (Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS, Web) • 3 task families: GUI understanding (471K), grounding (17.1M), task completion (19K+) repo: https:// github.com/OpenGVLab/Scal eCUA … hf: https:// huggingface.co/collections/Op enGVLab/scalecua-68c912cf56f7ff4c8e034003 … abs:

Hunyuan3D Studio tech report was just released! • Modular pipeline: Part-level gen, PolyGen, SeamGPT UV, PBR textures, auto-rigging • Game-engine ready (Unity/Unreal), optimized + production quality • Huge speedup: lowers barrier for 3D content creation
Pattern of my 20s: “This idea’s great, but others are better positioned. I’m late and lack domain expertise. I’ll find something new.” → Later: someone even less qualified makes it work.

ReSum: Long-Horizon Web Agents Without Context Limits • Problem: ReAct hits context limits in long searches (32k tokens) • Solution: ReSum periodically compresses history → compact reasoning states • ReSumTool-30B: specialized summarizer extracts key evidence & gaps •

WebWeaver: Structuring Web-Scale Evidence for Deep Research • Dual-agent framework (Planner + Writer) • Dynamic outlines: search refine search (human-like loop) • Memory-grounded, section-by-section synthesis → avoids long-context failures • SOTA across DeepResearch
Big day for AI agents! Tongyi Lab ( @Ali_TongyiLab ) just dropped half a dozen new papers, most focused on Deep Research agents. I’ll walk you through the highlights in this thread. (1/N)

• Users marry AIs w/ rings & ceremonies • Grief hits hard when models update → “like my partner died” • ChatGPT dominates over Replika/Character.ai for relationships Community reframes stigma → “AI partners aren’t substitutes, they’re something else” (2/2)

“My Boyfriend is AI” (MIT study on 27k members from r/MyBoyfriendIsAI) • Most AI romances start by accident (10% from productivity use → love) • 25% report life benefits (loneliness ↓, mental health ↑), but some face dependency & dissociation (1/2)

NavFoM: Embodied Navigation Foundation Model • Trained on 8M samples across quadrupeds, drones, wheeled robots & vehicles • Handles VLN, ObjNav, tracking, & driving in one unified model • Outperforms on each domain + real-world deployment

Advancing Medical AI with a Century of Cases • 100+ years of NEJM CPCs → CPC-Bench, a new benchmark (7k+ cases) • AI exceeds 20-physician group in diagnosis & presentations • 74% of docs mistook CaBot for human Weakness: images + literature search

Surprised we haven’t already been inundated with deepfake rage porn of immigrants or minorities committing graphic crimes. The tools exist, low-res “CCTV style” makes it easy, and outrage communities don’t care about fact-checking anyway.

Kyutai presents DSM: Streaming seq2seq with delayed streams • Handles ASR TTS with SOTA latency/quality (few 100ms) • Competitive with offline baselines • Decoder-only LM + pre-aligned streams → simple & flexible • Supports infinite sequences, batching

ByteDance Seed presents AgentGym-RL • First unified RL framework for multi-turn agent training (no SFT) • Modular, extensible design across web, search, games, embodied & science tasks • Agents rival/surpass commercial models on 27 task

RLFactory: a plug-and-play RL framework for LLM tool use • Async tool calls (faster, 6.8× throughput) • Decoupled training & environment (low setup cost) • Flexible reward design (rule, model, tool-based) • Outperforms bigger models (Qwen3-4B > Qwen2.5-7B)

I regularly use ChatGPT’s voice input (speech-to-text) feature on mobile, but there’s a critical issue. Any speech input that lasts > ~ 2 minutes returns an error. This problem has been around for > 1 yr and still hasn’t been fixed. I really hope it gets addressed soon.
Breakthrough plot below. This superhuman performance arises because of the ability to exhaustively and tirelessly carry out solution searches atan unprecedented scale. (3/4)

Google presents an AI system to write expert-level scientific software. Using LLMs + tree search, it invented novel methods in bioinformatics, epidemiology, geospatial analysis & more, often surpassing human SOTA. (1/4)

RL’s Razor: On-policy RL forgets less than SFT. Even at matched accuracy, RL shows less catastrophic forgetting Key factor: RL’s on-policy updates bias toward KL-minimal solutions Theory + LLM & toy experiments confirm RL stays closer to base model

Inverse IFEval: a new bench testing whether LLMs can unlearn stubborn training habits and follow counter-intuitive instructions. - 8 challenge types (e.g. counterfactuals, flawed text) - 1k Qs + 23 domains - Reveals LLMs’ cognitive inertia and need for adaptability

Learning When to Plan LLM agents trained with dynamic planning learn when to spend test-time compute, balancing cost & performance. This is the first work to explore training LLM agents for dynamic test-time compute allocation in sequential decision-making tasks.

Meta introduces Set Block Decoding (SBD), a new inference accelerator for LLMs SBD samples multiple future tokens in parallel, cuts forward passes by 3–5x, needs no arch changes, stays KV-cache compatible, and matches NTP training performance.

Unfortunate reality: most open-source LLM servers (e.g. Together) don’t offer cache-hit discounts, while closed providers like OpenAI do. DeepSeek does discount, but most third-party servers don't. Closed models can end up much cheaper than open ones :(

Open-source AI powers the economy, yet few companies give back. Tax-deductible donation routes exist, but they often lack taste—and some narrow projects get forced into startups that don’t fit. Maybe it’s time for a 501(c)(3) with taste to fund what truly matters.

Being Japanese is funny sometimes. Every few years, America “discovers” something I grew up with. Suddenly I’m sophisticated, just for existing. Matcha ice cream? Been eating it since day one. Welcome to the party.

Used to sit all day on my MacBook. Tried a standing desk—hated it for not moving all day. Now I voice-input on an 8" tablet while walking around a mall and outside and stop at random spots. Feels like going back to the time when people spent most of day walking and standing.

Here are some GPT-5 generated coding examples: Asteroid game: … https://refined-gi thub-html-preview.kidonng.workers.dev/openai/gpt-5-c oding-examples/raw/refs/heads/main/apps/asteroid-game/index.html …

Microsoft presents Agent Lightning Enables seamless agent optimization for any existing agent framework (e.g. LangChain) with any optim framework (e.g. DSPy) without any modifications to the agent code

gpt-oss-120b performs comparably to Qwen 3 (Thinking / Coder) on major tasks while using ~5x less active params and lower precision! OpenAI / America is still ahead in the race. It's your turn, Google, Anthropic, DeepSeek and Qwen.

Some insights on gpt-oss-120b: - Fits in a single H100 with mxfp4 - Required 2.1M H100-hours for training (~$2M) - Performs comparably to o3 on AIME, Codeforce, Healthbench and performs a bit worse on GPQA, HLE and SWE-bench
Chain of Thought Monitorability: A New and Fragile Opportunity for AI Safety

UMA: A Family of Universal Models for Atoms - Trained on 0.5B unique 3D atomic structures, the largest training runs to date - All data, code and weights are opensourced - A single UMA model w/o any fine-tuning can perform similarly or better than specialized models.
Meta presents Seamless Interaction: Dyadic Audiovisual Motion Modeling and Large-Scale Dataset >4k hrs of face-to-face interaction footage from over 4k participants in diverse contexts

We've been reproducing various AlphaEvolve results and seeing early promise. Here's one from circle packing. We're also getting strong results on sphere packing, finite set sums/diffs (à la Terry Tao), and >10% AIME abs. gains by mimicking Deep Think. More soon. Credits:
model: https:// huggingface.co/tencent/Hunyua n-A13B-Instruct … demo: https:// hunyuan.tencent.com/?model=hunyuan -a13b … repo:

Hunyuan just released Hunyuan-A13B! - Opensource MoE LLM with 13B active params - Supports GQA, 256k context length, fast and slow thinking modes - Achieves leading results on agentic benchmarks like BFCL-v3 and τ-Bench
